channel.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers" - Have you joined the site because you're an avid reader and are planning to vote, comment, or just lurk on the site? Interested in helping develop the Next Best Thing? Post your introduction here!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hi
I'm new to the site and decide to join after wanting to hopefully one day write a successful scp article! My account name is Codey Finn, and right now id love some good o'l swiss roll cake…

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hello!

I'm Comrade_rusky, i've been a fan of SCP for a few years now, i've played the games and read the posts and finally felt it time to contribute! Also I love the whole SCP theme of secrecy around creepy government stuff.

For the question of the month, I would say a nacho cheese plate to share.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hi!
RopesAndHopes here! Can call me Ropes or Rops because it's shorter.

I have been a fan of the SCP-verse for SO LONG and I decided that I want to contribute by reading, voting and commenting on articles. Not sure if I want to write articles for now, so I will stick to reading :)

For the random question of the month: best food to eat with friends is definitely pizza. I am probably biased as whenever my friends and I gather for socialising nights we get pizza.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hi,

I’m Manseaweed, been following SCP stuff for quite awhile now and thought I should go ahead and make an account.

As for food, I’ve enjoyed some good quality ramen with friends.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hi! Nightshade210 here. I've been lurking on the site for a while, but finally built up the courage to actually join the forums. I really like writing and brainstorming concepts, but I'm pretty sure it'll take a while until I post any writing here (if I ever do, you know, finish any of them). English isn't my first language, so if anything I write is confusing in any measure, please let me know! I'll appreciate it!

Best food to share is pizza. Period.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
'mornin fellas, call me Zetros. I've known about the wiki for a few years now, but only read a little bit back then, but decided to start reading once again after watching the Overlord short film (which was very good!). I'm probably gonna just reserve myself to reading, although I wouldn't mind dabbling in writing an SCP someday. Maybe not. Who knows. That being said I'm pretty interested in cars, I spend most of my time (and money!) turning wrenches and attempting to do janky solutions then finding out that maybe using zipties to fix something isn't quite the best way to do it :P

That being said, if any one of y'all writers have any questions regarding automobile function in a tale or SCP page, feel free to ask me for a hand. I'm sure I can lend a hand :)

Best food to eat with friends is chain pizza. Can't be the fancy stuff, only Dominoes or co. on a whim at 1am.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hello World! My username is DeadRainMan. I found the site after viewing several YouTube videos about the foundation. Comically it took me quite a while to realize these writings are all fiction.

What my main goal with joining the website is to fix spelling and grammatical errors in articles i read on the site, but I will probably spend most of my time reading the stories and asking questions in the forums.

To answer the random question, no food is better than Italian spaghetti.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hello, my name is Cecil. I've been lurking on the wiki anonymously for quite a while now, ever since two years ago, Volgun posted that video about SCP-1504 and it snuck into my recommendations. I enjoy drawing and long lists. Also classifications, they're neat. I think I'll continue just reading for now, I don't think I have enough knowledge to critique or write yet. I plan on doing that in the future, though.

Ice cream, honestly. Infinite tastes, infinite options. Gives you a feeling of 343-like power. Also, everyone can find a taste that suits them or something I guess

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hi! I'm Eltrafix

I've been reading on the SCP wiki for a few years and finally decided to join. I've written in the past and I'm hoping to write some SCP's in the future. I'll stay a reader for the time being though.

Food to share: Sushi, hotpot or tabletop grilling - wholesome and fun until the food runs out and mockfights ensue :P

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hey.
Soleven here, but you can call me Sol.

After thinking about it for a long time, I decided to finally join the wiki. Not quite sure what exactly I want to do here, for now I will focus on voting and commenting articles.

I think waffles are great. Or steak I would take both.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hello I'm Locust Preacher

I'm usually very interested in fictionel works with endless lore and worldbuilding. After my friend recommended The SCP Foundation I started to learn about it via Youtube but never really checked out the official site until a few days ago and here I am.

For the question: I have to go with grilled cheese sandwich. I honestly may have devoloped some kind of addiction

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hello, I'm brokenTower, I've read the articles on this site for around a decade and decided to get an account.

I'm American, so heavy bread is magic to me. It's bread, but filling!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hi. Been lurking for a few years now. No immediate plans to do anything but continue lurking, but I thought to create an account in case I change my mind.

For the random question, I'm surprised that there aren't more people saying pizza. Is it just too obvious?

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hello, I’m Katja. I’ve been keeping reading the scipps for many years but I’ve never been a member of this site until now. Finally I can vote those my favorite scipps. Hope the Foundation gets better and better.

As for the question of this month, I may choose a hotpot or BBQ. I LOVE THEM!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hi! I'm blcheung99!
I first learned about the Foundation a few years ago and ever since I've been absolutely fascinated by it. I figured the new year was as good a time as any to finally officially join the site! I'm probably still going to lurk for a while, but who knows?
As for the question of the month, you can never go wrong with a good Korean BBQ!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hello! I’m RelaxedRac!
I’ve explored the SCP wiki for a long time and finally decided to join officially. I even use some of the sweeter ones as bedtime stories! As for the question of the month, I’m always team curry!

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hello, I'm Jajastar9.
I joined this site as I am an enthusiastic fan of the SCP universe, and to possibly interact with other members

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hello, I'm Conn. :)
I joined this site through simply exploring the internet after finding out what SCPs were through a friends(and my own) curiosity.
I hope to one day write an SCP of my own, yet it will take some time. I also hope to, one day, read as many articles as possible, and support others who are writing.
A few hobbies I enjoy are drawing and writing, although I'm still practicing my writing. I also like horror themes, such as movies or books.

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded: Psst… there's at least one person every month who doesn't read the blue text in the top post! Please read the blue text!

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, readers and reviewers of the Foundation Universe!

This month's random question is: what do you think is the best kind of food to eat with friends?

Let's start off with some things to take a look at to enhance your reading, now that you're a site member:

  • If you have any questions about the Foundation'verse and its various individuals and groups of interest, head over to the Foundation Universe forum to talk about the various aspects of living/working/being in constant fear of a world where anomalies are real.
  • Did you know our site has a night mode version? If you're interested in that (and a few other neat things!), check out our Usertools page.
  • Want to leave a comment or read others' thoughts on something you've read recently? Scroll down to the bottom of that page you read, click the "Discuss" link, and take a look at the comments others have posted!

For those looking to put your knowledge of the SCP-verse to work and contribute to the site by helping develop works-in-progress, head on over to the Writing Help forums!

  • So you want to be a concept reviewer?
  • So you want to be a draft reviewer?
  • And of course, remember the Criticism Policy!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Go ahead and respond to other new members if you find that your interests overlap, and definitely ask for reading suggestions if there's a particular kind of SCP article or tale you enjoy reading. If you're looking to get started as a critiquer, feel free to mention the kind of material you particularly want to see more of on the wiki! You might even see some of our authors drop by here from time to time, if you mention you liked their work! :)

Welcome to the site!
